source.title: Demo reviewed right lower quadrant pain case
source.citation: Local reviewed educational case file for Diagnostician demo content.
source.raw_text: De-identified educational case source assembled and reviewed for demo validation.

case.title: Migrating abdominal pain with anorexia
teaching_points: Pain migration, anorexia, focal right lower quadrant tenderness, and inflammatory markers support the final diagnosis.
A normal urinalysis can help redirect away from urinary causes when the abdominal exam localizes.
Imaging should be interpreted with the clinical pattern rather than used as an isolated answer.

facts.label: Opening concern
facts.value: The patient reports abdominal pain with nausea and poor appetite.

provenance.quote: Abdominal pain with nausea and anorexia.

facts.label: Pain migration
facts.value: The pain began near the umbilicus overnight and moved to the right lower abdomen by morning.

provenance.quote: Periumbilical pain migrated to the right lower quadrant.

facts.label: Associated symptoms
facts.value: He has nausea and one episode of non-bloody emesis, without diarrhea.

provenance.quote: Nausea, one non-bloody emesis, no diarrhea.

facts.label: Surgical history
facts.value: He has no prior abdominal operations and no known inflammatory bowel disease.

provenance.quote: No prior abdominal surgery; no known IBD.

facts.label: Triage vitals
facts.value: Temperature is 38.1 C, heart rate 104/min, blood pressure 118/72 mmHg, respiratory rate 18/min, oxygen saturation 99% on room air.

facts.label: Abdominal exam
facts.value: The abdomen is soft but focally tender in the right lower quadrant with mild guarding.

provenance.quote: Soft abdomen, RLQ tenderness, mild guarding.

facts.label: CBC
facts.value: White blood cell count is 14.6 x 10^9/L with neutrophil predominance.

facts.label: Urinalysis
facts.value: Urinalysis shows no nitrites, leukocyte esterase, or hematuria.

provenance.quote: UA negative for nitrites, leukocyte esterase, and blood.

facts.label: CT abdomen and pelvis
facts.value: CT shows an enlarged blind-ending tubular structure in the right lower quadrant with surrounding fat stranding and no abscess.

provenance.quote: Enlarged appendix with periappendiceal fat stranding; no abscess.

facts.label: Dangerous differential
facts.value: A time-sensitive surgical abdominal process should remain high on the differential with this pattern.

provenance.quote: RLQ pain with guarding and leukocytosis supports urgent surgical consideration.

facts.label: Hint 1
facts.value: Track how the location of the pain changed and connect that to the focal exam finding.

provenance.quote: Pain migration plus focal RLQ tenderness is the key pattern.

facts.label: Final diagnosis
facts.value: Acute appendicitis supported by migrating pain, focal right lower quadrant tenderness, leukocytosis, and CT findings.
